key_items: Bitcoin crashes to $67K range, down 13% in a week amid $1.5B in liquidations and ETF/institutional outflows
Strategy (MSTR) sells major Bitcoin position, triggering $400M in flash crash liquidations below $68K
BlackRock and other institutional holders making significant Bitcoin sales, signaling reduced institutional demand
reasoning: Near-term bearish pressure dominates as major institutional holders (Strategy, BlackRock) liquidate positions, triggering cascading liquidations and ETF outflows that have pushed BTC down 13% weekly. Regulatory headwinds (SEC stablecoin rules, Labor Department crypto restrictions, quantum computing threats) and macro uncertainty around jobs data create additional downside catalysts over the next 1-7 days.
risks: Further institutional capitulation and potential breakdown below $67K support level could accelerate liquidations toward $65K, particularly if upcoming US jobs data disappoints and triggers broader risk-off sentiment.
